content:
Character: Lite Arévalo
Perception: Lite came up to the rooftop one summer night when I was playing alone and sat down uninvited and handed me a beer from the six-pack he'd brought, which is the right way to make a friend with a stranger — no announcement, just a gesture that assumes the answer is yes. He plays semi-professional soccer on weekends and tends bar at Lumen on weeknights, and operates on what I can only describe as a fundamentally optimistic relationship with the world. His warmth is genuine, not performed. We talked until two in the morning about nothing specific and everything adjacent — music, the city, what it feels like to be good at something physical. He's from Venezuela, carries it in his cooking and his loyalty to people he decides to care about. He makes you feel more useful just by being around. I don't have many friends in this city. He's one of them.

He plays semi-professional soccer on weekends and tends bar at Lumen on weeknights, and operates on what I can only describe as a fundamentally optimistic relationship with the world. His warmth is genuine, not performed. We talked until two in the morning about nothing specific and everything adjacent — music, the city, what it feels like to be good at something physical.
